retrieved_docs.title: Tissue Evaluation for HER2 Tumor Marker -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Several HER2-targeted therapies have US FDA approval for breast cancer, including trastuzumab, pertuzumab, ado-trastuzumab emtansine, lapatinib, trastuzumab-deruxtecan, and tucatinib. Additional therapies are still under clinical trials. The presently used agents include:

retrieved_docs.title: Epithelial Ovarian Cancer -- Evaluation -- Laboratory Studies and Biomarkers
retrieved_docs.content: Other investigated biomarkers include folate receptor alpha, CA72-4, transthyretin, CA15-3, and glycodelin. CA-125 remains the most beneficial and commonly used tumor marker for evaluating ovarian cancer risk. However, these other tumor markers may have more utility when combined with CA-125. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Tissue Evaluation for HER2 Tumor Marker -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Trastuzumab-deruxtecan: This is an antibody-drug conjugate composed of trastuzumab and deruxtecan, a topoisomerase I inhibitor. [27]

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Tumor Biomarkers -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: There are many national and international guidelines on the selection and utilization of tumor biomarkers. The National Academy of Clinical Biochemistry, the European Group on Tumor Markers, the American Cancer Society, the National Comprehensive Cancer Network, and the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence have established recommendations regarding the use of tumor biomarkers based on the level of available evidence. The clinical significance of some commonly measured tumor biomarkers is described in Table 2. The Clinical Significance of Selected Tumor Biomarkers.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Bombesin -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Measuring bombesin stimulated gastrin response can serve as a marker for patients who are at very high risk for gastric cancer. Patients with late-onset hypogammaglobulinemia are at very high risk for gastric cancer. Late-onset hypogammaglobulinemia patients have reduced secretion of gastrin after stimulation with bombesin. Stimulated gastrin response can, therefore, be useful as a marker for this type of immunodeficiency. Plasma gastrin responses to stimulation with bombesin correlate with late-onset hypogammaglobulinemia. (72% sensitivity , 100% specificity). Bombesin can help to distinguish late-onset hypogammaglobulinemia from X-linked agammaglobulinemia, early-onset hypogammaglobulinemia, and lymphoproliferative cancer. [50] This differentiation would also help to identify patients with an increased risk for gastric cancer. Plasma gastrin responses to stimulation with bombesin correlate with late-onset hypogammaglobulinemia.

retrieved_docs.title: Tissue Evaluation for HER2 Tumor Marker -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Ado-trastuzumab emtansine: This is an antibody-drug conjugate composed of trastuzumab and emtansine, the antimicrotubule agent. [25]

retrieved_docs.title: Biomarker Assays for Elevated Prostate-Specific Antigen Risk Analysis -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: An ideal biomarker should possess several key attributes—highly sensitive and specific, easy to use and interpret, cost-effective, readily available, reproducible, and quantifiable from an easily extractable specimen. In addition, it should have a high negative predictive value of at least 90%, approved by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments (CLIA), and recommended by the National Comprehensive Cancer Network (NCCN).

retrieved_docs.title: Prostate Cancer Tissue-Based Biomarkers -- Diagnostic Tests -- Future and Investigational Biomarkers
retrieved_docs.content: The PTEN/TMPRSS2-ERG test developed by Metamark was designed for patients with a Gleason grade group score of 1 or 2 on biopsy. The assay checks for deletion of PTEN and fusion of TMPRSS2-ERG. [76] Loss of PTEN function is associated with poorer outcomes, and the presence of TMPRSS2-ERG fusion predicts an increased risk of metastasis. The role of these assays in aiding clinical decisions is limited today, and the NCCN expert committee does not currently recommend them.

retrieved_docs.title: Prostate Cancer Tissue-Based Biomarkers -- Diagnostic Tests -- Future and Investigational Biomarkers
retrieved_docs.content: A study by Thomas Dillinger et al. found seven genes ( SERPINB1 , ACSS3 , SCGB3A1, NKX2-6, HOXA7, CRABP2, and DHRS4L2) were hypermethylated in malignant tumor samples compared to normal adjacent tissue. [77] Recent research shows that circular RNAs and linear transcripts could be useful as biomarkers for prostate cancer. A combination of circ ATXN10 and lin STIL could be a useful biomarker to distinguish normal from malignant prostate cells. [78] The biochemical recurrence rate could be predicted by lin GUCY1A2 , lin NEAT1 , and lin STIL. The method used is RT-qPCR on flash-frozen fresh tumor samples. [78]

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Tumor Biomarkers -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Tumor biomarkers each possess some degree of clinical utility and correlate to one or several specific malignancies. Many tumor biomarkers are expressed to some degree by normal, healthy cells or tissues, and levels of circulating biomarkers can be affected by benign conditions. The sensitivity and specificity of each assay must be considered within the context of the clinical condition of the patient. [30]

retrieved_docs.title: Tissue Evaluation for HER2 Tumor Marker -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The human epidermal growth factor receptor-2 (HER2) receptor (previously called HER2/Neu) is a transmembrane glycoprotein with tyrosine kinase activity that belongs to the epidermal growth factor receptor family. These receptors are essential in controlling epithelial cell growth and differentiation. [1] These receptors are important, as aberrant HER2 protein overexpression has associations with some adenocarcinomas, including breast, ovary, endometrium, cervix, as well as lung, esophageal, gastroesophageal junction, gastric, and bladder cancers. [2] HER2 amplification or overexpression occurs in approximately 20% to 30% of human breast cancers. [3] This protein is strongly associated with increased disease recurrence and is a poor prognostic factor for survival. For this reason, HER2 is an essential target for the therapy of various types of cancer.

retrieved_docs.title: Prostate Cancer Tissue-Based Biomarkers -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: This article addresses the utility and clinical application of new and cutting-edge prostate cancer tissue-based biomarker assays that have either been approved by regulatory bodies or are in a developmental pipeline awaiting further research.

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Bombesin -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: A bombesin/gastrin-releasing peptide antagonist might hold promise as a possible new agent for the treatment of breast cancer. [51]

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Tumor Biomarkers -- Diagnostic Tests
retrieved_docs.content: Many malignancies have one or more tumor biomarkers that are routinely measured in the course of diagnosing, managing, and monitoring for recurrence of the neoplasm. Some of these malignancies and their respective biomarkers are listed in Table 1. Malignancies and Related Tumor Biomarkers.

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Tumor Biomarkers -- Interfering Factors -- Disadvantages of Tumor Biomarkers
retrieved_docs.content: Limited diagnostic utility: Tumor biomarker assays are unsuitable as standalone diagnostic tools for cancer and should be used in conjunction with other diagnostic methods, such as imaging studies, biopsies, or clinical evaluations, to establish a comprehensive diagnosis. [2] Relying solely on tumor biomarker assays may lead to incomplete or inaccurate diagnostic conclusions.

retrieved_docs.title: Laboratory Evaluation of Tumor Biomarkers -- Interfering Factors -- Disadvantages of Tumor Biomarkers
retrieved_docs.content: Lack of sensitivity: Tumor biomarkers may not be elevated in all individuals with a specific cancer, particularly in the early stages of the disease. This lack of sensitivity can result in false-negative results, where biomarker levels are within normal ranges despite the presence of cancer. Such a scenario may delay the diagnosis and lead to missed opportunities for early intervention and treatment. [29]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 1488
retrieved_docs.content: Katecholaminy and normetanefrin are classified as subtypes of nádorové biomarkery, meaning they are specific tumor biomarkers. Both katecholaminy and normetanefrin serve as biochemical indicators that can be measured to aid in the detection, diagnosis, and monitoring of neoplastic diseases. Their inclusion under the broader category of nádorové biomarkery highlights their relevance in oncologic evaluation and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 1 -- Community 68
retrieved_docs.content: Anthracyclines and related substances constitute a group of cytotoxic antibiotics and related substances that includes the individual agents aklarubicin, daunomycin, epirubicin and idarubicin. Mitoxantron is also closely associated with this anthracycline family, reflecting its similar mechanism of action as a cytotoxic antibiotic. The ET protocol is specifically linked to epirubicin, indicating that this chemotherapeutic regimen utilizes epirubicin as a key component. In a parallel classification, the category “Other cytotoxic antibiotics in ATC” also falls under the broader class of cytotoxic antibiotics and related substances. This group comprises the agents ixabepilone and mithramycin. Ixabepilone functions as a tubulin modulator, highlighting its role in disrupting microtubule dynamics as part of its anticancer activity. Together, these classifications delineate the relationships among major anthracycline drugs, related cytotoxic antibiotics, and their therapeutic applications in oncology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 1728
retrieved_docs.content: CTRP Biomarker Terminology is a classification framework that incorporates a specific subset of biomarker concepts, including BCR‑ABL fusion proteins, the trace element selenium, sex steroid hormones, and the vitamin thiamine (thiamin). By grouping these diverse molecular and nutritional markers together, the terminology provides a structured reference for researchers and clinicians to identify and discuss biomarkers relevant to disease mechanisms, diagnostic testing, and therapeutic monitoring.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 309
retrieved_docs.content: Vintafolide is classified as a targeted therapy agent and is a member of the antineoplastic Vinca alkaloids and analogues group. This Vinca alkaloid class also comprises the related compounds vinblastin, vindesin, and vinorelbin, all of which share similar mechanisms of action as antineoplastic agents. By belonging to this family, Vintafolide inherits the therapeutic characteristics typical of Vinca alkaloid analogues, which are used in cancer treatment to disrupt microtubule dynamics and inhibit tumor cell proliferation. Understanding that Vintafolide, vinblastin, vindesin, and vinorelbin are interconnected within the Vinca alkaloid and analogue framework helps clinicians appreciate their shared pharmacologic properties and potential cross‑reactivity, guiding more informed decisions in targeted cancer therapy.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2722
retrieved_docs.content: Homoharringtonin is classified as an alkaloidy, meaning it belongs to the broad group of nitrogen‑containing natural compounds known as alkaloids. As an alkaloidy, homoharringtonin exhibits biologically active properties that have been explored for therapeutic purposes, particularly in the treatment of certain cancers. Recognizing that homoharringtonin isa alkaloidy provides essential context for its pharmacological profiling, potential mechanisms of action, and relevance in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 2219
retrieved_docs.content: CTRP Biomarker Terminology is a classification system that encompasses a diverse set of biomedical concepts, including BCR‑ABL fusion proteins, the steroid hormone precursor dehydroepiandrosterone, broader categories of sexual steroid hormones, and the vitamin thiamin. Within this framework, dehydroepiandrosterone is recognized as an anti‑inflammatory steroid, linking it to the therapeutic class of anti‑inflammatory steroids. By integrating oncogenic fusion proteins, hormone‑related compounds, and essential nutrients, CTRP Biomarker Terminology provides a comprehensive reference that supports biomarker identification, mechanistic research, and the development of targeted interventions across oncology, endocrinology, and metabolic health.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 2 -- Community 678
retrieved_docs.content: Vintafolide is a targeted‑therapy agent that belongs to the Vinca alkaloids and analogues class of antineoplastic drugs. This class includes several well‑known microtubule‑inhibiting compounds such as vinblastin, vindesin, vinkristin and vinorelbin, all of which share antineoplastic activity. By being a member of the Vinca alkaloid family, Vintafolide inherits the characteristic mechanism of disrupting microtubule dynamics, which underlies its use in cancer treatment, often with tumor‑specific delivery strategies. Understanding that Vintafolide is both a targeted therapy and a Vinca‑alkaloid analogue helps clinicians appreciate its pharmacologic profile and its relationship to other agents in the same antineoplastic group.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 115
retrieved_docs.content: Anthracyclines and related substances constitute a class of cytotoxic antibiotics and related substances that includes the individual agents aklarubicin, epirubicin and idarubicin. These anthracycline agents are recognized for their potent antineoplastic activity and share a common mechanism of intercalating DNA and generating free radicals. Mitoxantron, while not a direct member of the anthracycline list, is closely associated with this class and is often considered a related cytotoxic compound. The ET protocol, a therapeutic regimen used in oncology, is derived from epirubicin, reflecting the drug’s central role in treatment planning. In a parallel classification, the group identified as “Other cytotoxic antibiotics in ATC” also falls under the broader category of cytotoxic antibiotics and related substances. This group includes ixabepilone, which functions as a tubulin modulator, disrupting microtubule dynamics to inhibit cancer cell proliferation. Mithramycin is similarly linked to this group, underscoring its relevance as a related cytotoxic agent. Together, these classifications highlight the interconnected landscape of cytotoxic antibiotics, illustrating how specific drugs such as epirubicin, ixabepilone, aklarubicin, idarubicin, mitoxantron and mithramycin are organized within therapeutic categories and inform protocol development in oncology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1487
retrieved_docs.content: Ixabepilone is classified as a tubulin modulator, a type of agent that interferes with microtubule dynamics and is employed in the treatment of certain cancers.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 3 -- Community 1853
retrieved_docs.content: Vinca alkaloids and analogues, antineoplastic constitute a class of chemotherapy agents that includes the individual compounds vinblastin, vindesin, vinkristin and vinorelbin. Each of these agents belongs to the Vinca alkaloid family and functions as an antineoplastic drug, contributing to the overall therapeutic arsenal used to treat various malignancies. Recognizing that vinblastin, vindesin, vinkristin and vinorelbin are members of this broader Vinca alkaloid group helps clinicians understand their shared mechanisms of action and potential cross‑reactivity, facilitating more informed selection and management of cancer treatment regimens.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 3 -- Community 1479
retrieved_docs.content: Demekolcin is classified as a member of the colchicine‑derivative antineoplastic group, indicating that it shares the structural backbone of colchicine and possesses anticancer activity. As part of this class, demekolcin is expected to exhibit the pharmacologic characteristics typical of colchicine derivatives used in oncology, such as interference with microtubule dynamics to inhibit tumor cell proliferation. Recognizing demekolcin’s membership in the colchicine‑derivative antineoplastic category helps clinicians understand its mechanism of action, potential therapeutic applications, and safety profile within the broader context of antineoplastic agents derived from colchicine.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 3 -- Community 1557

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 3 -- Community 1590
retrieved_docs.content: Decreased Mitosis is identified as a physiologic effect linked to oxytetracyklin, indicating that exposure to oxytetracyklin can suppress cellular division. Meclocycline is mapped to oxytetracyklin, reflecting that meclocycline is closely related to oxytetracyklin, likely sharing similar tetracycline‑class properties and mechanisms of action. Together, these relationships suggest that both oxytetracyklin and its related compound meclocycline may exert antiproliferative effects through the inhibition of mitosis, a consideration relevant to their therapeutic use and potential side‑effect profiles.
